headline: The Defensive Circus
storySoFar: Scraped a point against Canada, but absolutely imploded against Switzerland. Two yellows and a straight red in a 20-minute spell turned their defense into a circus in a 4-1 loss.
whatsNext: Need to figure out how to keep 11 men on the pitch if they want any realistic chance of progression.
pubAmmo: Did you know Bosnia managed to tackle themselves out of the Switzerland game by collecting three cards between the 59th and 80th minutes?

headline: Slightly Better Than Terrible, But Barely
storySoFar: Scraped by on penalties against Denmark. Absolute Sunday-League coordination in the middle with sideways passing that would put a caffeine addict to sleep.
whatsNext: Need to stop relying on luck or they will be on the first flight home before the postcards arrive.
pubAmmo: Did you know England have dropped more points from winning positions than any other European team in World Cup history?

headline: The Bravehearts' Liquid Devastation
storySoFar: An absolute masterclass in tactical stubbornness. Parked a double-decker bus against Morocco, only to concede to an overhead kick that defied all laws of Scottish gravity.
whatsNext: Need to find a forward who can run without looking like he's wading through wet porridge.
pubAmmo: Did you know Scotland have played in 8 World Cups and never once made it past the first round? Pain is an art form.

headline: Atlas Lions on the Rampage
storySoFar: Liquid football from the Moroccans. Slick one-touches that made the Scottish defense spin like laundry. Hakimi's cross for the winner belonged in the Louvre.
whatsNext: Need to tighten up the backline; they were caught sleeping twice on simple long balls.
pubAmmo: Did you know Morocco became the first African nation to reach a World Cup semi-final, and they've kept more clean sheets than any other African team?

headline: Star-Spangled Swagger
storySoFar: Smooth control and high-intensity press. Completely rattled Australia with direct running. Christian Pulisic was a walking cheat code, dribbling past defenders as if they were training cones.
whatsNext: Must maintain this intensity in the knockouts; complacency is the only real enemy.
pubAmmo: Did you know the USA's 3-0 win against Paraguay in 1930 featured the first-ever hat-trick in World Cup history by Bert Patenaude?

headline: Socceroos' Squeaky Bum Time
storySoFar: An absolute horror show in possession. Handed the ball to the Americans on a silver platter. Completely bottled it in the second half with static defending.
whatsNext: Need to go back to basic passing drills and stop panicking when pressed.
pubAmmo: Did you know Australia's nickname 'Socceroos' was coined in 1967 by a journalist named Tony Horstead?

headline: Samba Style or Casual Kickabout?
storySoFar: Breezed past Haiti with Vinicius Jr playing like he was in his back garden. 5-0 looks good, but they missed enough sitters to warrant a stern dressing down.
whatsNext: Need to keep their feet on the ground and show some respect for the offside rule.
pubAmmo: Did you know Brazil is the only country to have played in every single World Cup tournament?

headline: Haiti's World Cup Hopes Dashed, Officially Eliminated
storySoFar: Haiti's World Cup campaign has been a tough one, with two losses and zero points. They've struggled to make an impact in Group C.
whatsNext: They are officially eliminated. Their maximum possible points won't be enough to reach the next stage, so it's an early flight home.
pubAmmo: Haiti are the first team from Group C to be eliminated, proving that sometimes, the numbers just don't lie.

headline: The Snarl and the Sweep
storySoFar: Paraguay's physical display contrasted heavily with Turkey's smooth control. Relentless tackling that bordered on a contact sport. Galarza scored after 64 seconds.
whatsNext: Need to control their tempers; three yellow cards in the second half was playing with fire.
pubAmmo: Did you know Paraguay have reached the Round of 16 four times but only made the Quarterfinals once in 2010?

headline: The Swear-O-Meter Disaster
storySoFar: An absolute circus. Normal service was resumed until Almiron got sent off within forty minutes for swearing at the referee, completely derailing their defensive blueprint.
whatsNext: Need to teach their players the international sign language for silence before the next match.
pubAmmo: Did you know Turkey finished third in the 2002 World Cup, where Hakan Sükür scored the fastest goal in history (11 seconds)?

headline: Dutch Masters Demolish Sweden, Group F Looking Orange!
storySoFar: The Netherlands have been in fine form, and today's 5-1 thrashing of Sweden just cemented their place at the top of Group F. Seven points from three games, and a goal difference that's looking rather healthy.
whatsNext: With qualification all but secured, they can put their feet up and watch the rest of the group stage unfold. Job done, and done with style.
pubAmmo: The Netherlands have now scored 12 goals in three games, proving they're not just pretty passers, but proper goal machines!

headline: Sweden's World Cup Dream Dented by Dutch Demolition!
storySoFar: It was a tough day at the office for Sweden, who were absolutely battered by the Netherlands. Conceding five goals is never a good look, and they're now sitting third in Group F with just three points.
whatsNext: They'll be hoping for a miracle in the final round of fixtures, needing results elsewhere to go their way if they're to sneak into the knockout stages as a best third-placed team. It's squeaky bum time!
pubAmmo: Sweden conceded more goals in this one match (5) than they had in their previous two combined (6). Ouch.

headline: Germany Through! Undav's Late Heroics Secure Top Spot!
storySoFar: Three games, three wins for Germany! They left it late against Côte d'Ivoire, but Deniz Undav's brace ensured they maintained their perfect record and secured their passage to the knockouts as Group E winners.
whatsNext: The Germans can now relax, knowing they've topped their group. They'll be looking to maintain momentum and fine-tune things for the business end of the tournament.
pubAmmo: Germany has now scored a whopping 11 goals in the group stage, making them one of the most potent attacks in the competition. Clinical!

headline: Côte d'Ivoire's Valiant Effort Falls Short Against Germany!
storySoFar: Côte d'Ivoire put up a brave fight against the mighty Germans, even taking the lead, but ultimately succumbed to a late defeat. They're currently third in Group E with three points.
whatsNext: Their fate now hangs in the balance. They'll need to hope their three points and goal difference are enough to see them through as one of the best third-placed teams. It's out of their hands now.
pubAmmo: Despite the loss, Côte d'Ivoire managed to score against Germany, which is more than some teams can say! Small victories, eh?

headline: Ecuador's Goalless Grind Leaves Them in Limbo!
storySoFar: Another game, another draw, and still no goals for Ecuador! Their 0-0 stalemate with Curaçao leaves them stuck on two points in Group E, with their World Cup hopes looking rather bleak.
whatsNext: With only two points and zero goals scored, Ecuador are in a precarious position. They'll need a miracle and a calculator to figure out if they can still progress. Flights home might be on the cards.
pubAmmo: Ecuador has played three games and failed to score a single goal. You could say their attack is as potent as a wet paper bag.

headline: Curaçao Hold On for a Point, Eloy Room a Hero!
storySoFar: Curaçao managed to grind out a 0-0 draw against Ecuador, largely thanks to their heroic goalkeeper. They're still bottom of Group E with two points, but they showed some real grit.
whatsNext: While a point is a point, it's unlikely to be enough for Curaçao to progress. They'll be heading home, but with their heads held high after a valiant effort today.
pubAmmo: Curaçao's Eloy Room made an astonishing 15 saves today, single-handedly keeping his side in the game. Give that man a medal!

headline: Tunisia's World Cup Dream Goes Up in Smoke
storySoFar: Tunisia's World Cup campaign has been an absolute disaster, culminating in a 4-0 thrashing by Japan today. Zero goals scored in three games and a whopping 13 conceded. Ouch.
whatsNext: With 0 points after all three games, Tunisia are officially ELIMINATED and can start booking their flights home. Better luck next time, lads.
pubAmmo: Did you see Tunisia's defence today? More holes than a golf course, and about as much resistance as a wet paper bag.

headline: Japan Marches On! Clinical Performance Sees Them Qualify
storySoFar: Japan have been absolutely brilliant, topping their group with 7 points and a sensational goal difference. Today's 4-0 win was just another feather in their cap.
whatsNext: Japan are QUALIFIED for the next round! They'll be putting their feet up, watching the drama unfold elsewhere, and preparing for the knockout stages.
pubAmmo: Japan are playing some liquid football, aren't they? Fast, incisive, and they know where the back of the net is. Proper dark horses, I tell ya.

headline: Spain Cruises to Qualification with Another Dominant Display
storySoFar: Spain have been nothing short of magnificent, securing their qualification with a dominant 4-0 victory over Saudi Arabia. Seven points, eight goals scored, and not a single one conceded. Impressive stuff.
whatsNext: Spain are QUALIFIED! They've done the business and can now focus on who they'll be facing in the next round. Job done, and done well.
pubAmmo: Spain are just toying with teams, aren't they? All that possession, all those chances. It's like watching a masterclass in how to pass a football around.

headline: Saudi Arabia's World Cup Journey Ends in Disappointment
storySoFar: Saudi Arabia's World Cup campaign has been a tough one, ending today with a 4-0 defeat to Spain. They managed just one shot on target against Spain, which tells you all you need to know.
whatsNext: With just 1 point after all three games, Saudi Arabia are officially ELIMINATED. Time to pack their bags and reflect on what could have been.
pubAmmo: Saudi Arabia's defence today was like a revolving door. Everyone got a turn to walk through it, especially Spain's forwards.

headline: Belgium's Golden Generation Fails to Shine, Bows Out
storySoFar: Belgium's World Cup campaign has been a monumental disappointment, finishing with a drab 0-0 draw against Iran and a red card. Just one goal scored in three games is simply not good enough for a team of their supposed quality.
whatsNext: With only 3 points after all three games, Belgium are ELIMINATED. The 'Golden Generation' has completely bottled it and will be heading home. Absolute scenes.
pubAmmo: Belgium had more possession than a haunted house but couldn't scare a goal out of Iran. And a red card? Just sums up their tournament, doesn't it?

headline: Iran Secures Qualification with Gritty Draw
storySoFar: Iran have shown real resilience, grinding out a 0-0 draw against Belgium today to secure their spot in the next round. Three draws from three games, but it was enough!
whatsNext: Iran are QUALIFIED for the next round! They've done it the hard way, but they're through and can look forward to the knockout stages.
pubAmmo: Iran's keeper was a brick wall today, wasn't he? Pulled off some absolute worldies to keep Belgium out. Proper heroics.

headline: Uruguay Scrapes Through Despite Late Drama
storySoFar: Uruguay had a bit of a scare today, drawing 2-2 with Cape Verde, but it was enough to see them through. Three draws from three, but they're in the knockouts.
whatsNext: Uruguay are QUALIFIED for the next round! They'll need to tighten things up, but they've got the job done and can prepare for the challenges ahead.
pubAmmo: Uruguay looked a bit shaky at the back today, didn't they? Conceded a free-kick and then let a substitute score from outside the box. Not exactly solid.

headline: Cape Verde's Valiant Fight Ends in Elimination
storySoFar: Cape Verde put up a brave fight today, earning a 2-2 draw against Uruguay, but it wasn't enough to keep their World Cup dream alive. They showed spirit, but the points just weren't there.
whatsNext: With 3 points after all three games, Cape Verde are ELIMINATED. They gave it a good go, but it's time to head home. Chin up, lads.
pubAmmo: Cape Verde scored a cracking free-kick and had a super sub come on to score. They might be out, but they certainly didn't go quietly!

headline: New Zealand's World Cup Adventure Comes to an End
storySoFar: New Zealand gave it their all, even taking an early lead against Egypt, but ultimately fell to a 3-1 defeat. One point from three games means their journey is over.
whatsNext: With just 1 point after all three games, New Zealand are ELIMINATED. They showed some fight, but it wasn't enough. Time to start planning for the next one.
pubAmmo: New Zealand scored a header from a corner, which is always nice to see. Shame they couldn't hold onto the lead, eh?

headline: Egypt Powers Through to the Knockouts
storySoFar: Egypt showed their class today, coming from behind to beat New Zealand 3-1 and secure their spot in the next round. Seven points from three games, a solid performance.
whatsNext: Egypt are QUALIFIED for the next round! They've navigated the group stage with confidence and will be looking to make a deep run in the tournament.
pubAmmo: Mohamed Salah is just a different gravy, isn't he? Scores, assists, makes everything tick. A proper walking cheat code for Egypt.

headline: Argentina through, but Messi's penalty miss gave us all a fright!
storySoFar: Two wins from two, top of Group J, and looking rather comfortable. They're doing exactly what's expected of them, which is always a bit boring, isn't it?
whatsNext: Argentina have officially qualified for the next round. Time to put their feet up and enjoy the show, or at least try not to get any more unnecessary yellow cards.
pubAmmo: Messi missed a penalty! Yes, you heard that right. Even the GOAT has his off days, though he quickly made up for it. Still, imagine the banter in the dressing room.

headline: Austria's World Cup dream ends with a whimper against Argentina.
storySoFar: It's been a tough ride for Austria, managing only one win in a group that proved a bit too strong for them. Today's loss sealed their fate.
whatsNext: Austria are eliminated from the competition. Time to book those flights home, lads. Better luck next time, eh?
pubAmmo: They only managed one shot on target against Argentina. One! You'd get more shots on target in a Sunday League match after a few too many pints.

headline: France cruise into the knockouts, Mbappé still scoring for fun.
storySoFar: Three games, three wins, nine goals scored, and looking utterly dominant. They're making this World Cup business look far too easy.
whatsNext: France have qualified for the next round. They'll be looking to maintain this blistering form and send a message to the rest of the competition.
pubAmmo: Kylian Mbappé just keeps on scoring. It's almost unfair, isn't it? He's a walking highlight reel, and Iraq certainly felt the full force of it.

headline: Iraq bow out without a whimper, failing to register a single shot on target.
storySoFar: Three losses and zero points. It's been a World Cup to forget for Iraq, who struggled to make any impact whatsoever.
whatsNext: Iraq are eliminated from the competition. They'll be heading home with plenty to ponder, especially how to get a shot on target.
pubAmmo: Zero shots on target in a World Cup match. That's not just bad, that's 'did they even bring a football?' bad. A truly forgettable performance.

headline: Haaland powers Norway into the next round after a nervy win.
storySoFar: Three wins from three, ten goals scored, and a perfect record. They've shown they're not just a one-man team, but having Haaland certainly helps!
whatsNext: Norway have qualified for the next round. They'll be buzzing after securing their spot and will be looking to carry this momentum forward.
pubAmmo: Haaland scored two goals, because of course he did. The man's a machine, and he's dragging Norway through this tournament almost single-handedly.

headline: Senegal's World Cup journey ends despite Sarr's best efforts.
storySoFar: Three losses, zero points. It's been a tough group for Senegal, and despite showing flashes of brilliance, they just couldn't get over the line.
whatsNext: Senegal are eliminated from the competition. A disappointing end to their campaign, but they can hold their heads high for at least trying to make a game of it.
pubAmmo: They had more possession and more shots than Norway, but still lost. It's the classic 'all bark, no bite' scenario, isn't it?

headline: Jordan's brave fight ends as Algeria snatch late victory.
storySoFar: Three losses, zero points. They showed some spirit today, but ultimately, the quality of their opponents was just too much.
whatsNext: Jordan are eliminated from the competition. They gave it a go, but alas, it wasn't enough to keep their World Cup dream alive.
pubAmmo: Their goalkeeper made 6 saves, which tells you everything about how much pressure they were under. He earned his dinner tonight, the rest of them... less so.

headline: Algeria stage late comeback to secure qualification in dramatic fashion.
storySoFar: Two wins and a loss, finishing second in Group J. They've shown resilience and a knack for scoring when it matters, which is always a good sign.
whatsNext: Algeria have qualified for the next round. They'll be buzzing after that comeback and will be looking to build on this momentum.
pubAmmo: Coming back from a goal down with two late goals? That's the kind of drama we love to see! They certainly know how to make 'squeaky bum time' even squeakier.
